content:
Hi. When I implement for_each_*_cpus() in this PR, Ojeda suggested using a use case to show how to use the returned CPU index. I want to use the C macro DEFINE_PER_CPU to define a percpu variable and per_cpu to get this variable. Then the problem comes.

The DEFINE_PER_CPU macro needs a parameter type, which is a date type of the percpu variable. If I want to wrap this function in the helpers.c, I need to find a parameter capable of representing any data type. This cannot be achieved in C due to the lack of generic T. Therefore, I have to turn to another macro alloc_percpu(size, align) for help. It doesn't need a type parameter and is easy to implement. But this macro defines the percpu variables dynamically, while DEFINE_PER_CPU statically defines a percpu variable.

Is there a method to implement DEFINE_PER_CPU in rust rather than using alloc_percpu to replace it? Thanks for your help!
